affordances.name: Starting condition comparison gate
affordances.mechanism: When a recommendation claims improvement, change, or execution readiness, require the answer to name the shared starting condition, success criteria, and comparison facts before treating later movement as meaningful.

use_when: A team lacks a shared starting picture of the problem, the success criteria, or the facts that later comparisons should be judged against.
Diagnosis, measurement, and ownership depend on agreeing what normal, broken, or improved actually mean.
Later intervention quality will be judged by change from an explicit starting condition rather than by impressionistic anecdotes.
do_not_use_when: The initial baseline has fossilized into doctrine and the team keeps optimizing around an outdated starting frame after the environment has shifted.
The chosen baseline metric is convenient to measure but weakly tied to the real causal drivers of success or failure.
The proposed baseline defines a symptom rather than the root condition that future action should change.
case_evidence_needed: The desired outcome and success criteria.
The facts, metrics, or observations that define normal, broken, or improved.
The context shift that would require revisiting the baseline.

treatment_requirements.description: Require the answer to define what starting condition and comparison standard must exist before claims of improvement, diagnosis, or execution progress can be trusted.
evidence_required: The claimed improvement or decision target.
The starting facts or current operating condition.
The comparison metric or qualitative marker.
The causal reason that marker reflects the real problem.
good_output_shape: Defines what normal, broken, and improved mean.
Separates convenient measurement from causal relevance.
Names when the baseline should be revisited.

diagnostic_questions: What is the shared starting condition before this advice is judged?
What does normal, broken, or improved mean in this case?
Is the chosen baseline causally tied to success, or just easy to measure?
misuse_guards: Do not let an obsolete baseline become doctrine after context shifts.
Do not use a convenient metric as the baseline if it does not track the real driver.
Do not define the baseline around a symptom and then optimize the wrong condition.

source_evidence.source_quote: a team lacks a shared starting picture of the problem, the success criteria, or the facts that later comparisons should be judged against
change from an explicit starting condition rather than by impressionistic anecdotes

review_notes.normalization_note: Batch 8 outcome: strong_affordance_record. The source supports one operational baseline gate for starting facts, success criteria, comparison meaning, and measurement discipline.
dropped_material: Did not extract AI persona consistency, TOSCA/design brief, or commander's intent as separate affordances.
Kept goal/process separation as context for baseline discipline.
open_questions: PR40 should test whether this card prevents packet recommendations from claiming improvement without a starting condition.

affordances.name: Absolute yardstick before contrast
affordances.mechanism: When an answer accepts an option because it looks better than an adjacent alternative, require an independent yardstick, measurement signal, or go/no-go standard before treating the contrast as decision evidence.

use_when: A recommendation is justified because it is better than a nearby option, scary alternative, prior offer, peer comparison, or current anchor.
The answer has not named the independent standard that would still govern if the comparison frame disappeared.
The decision needs an absolute threshold, measurement signal, or baseline condition before a relative improvement should change action.
do_not_use_when: The case already states a decision standard, threshold, and measurement signal that are independent of the comparison frame.
The right move is broad multi-criteria trade-off analysis rather than a missing absolute yardstick.
The comparison is only illustrative and does not carry the recommendation.
case_evidence_needed: The contrast frame, anchor, alternative, or scary comparator making the option look acceptable.
The user's goal or success criterion.
The independent yardstick, threshold, or measurement signal that should govern the decision.
The action that changes if the option fails the yardstick even though it wins the comparison.

treatment_requirements.description: Require the answer to name the yardstick, threshold, or measurement signal that governs the choice before endorsing an option because it looks better than a comparator.
evidence_required: The comparator or anchor driving the contrast.
The decision goal or success criterion.
The consequence if the option beats the comparator but fails the yardstick.
good_output_shape: States the independent yardstick in plain language.
Gives a go/no-go or revise condition tied to the yardstick.

diagnostic_questions: What yardstick would still govern if the comparison option disappeared?
Is this option good enough for the goal, or only better than the nearby alternative?
What measurement signal or threshold would make the contrast irrelevant?
misuse_guards: Do not turn every ordinary option comparison into a demand for new metrics.
Do not accept a vague standard such as fair, reasonable, or better without a threshold the user can apply.
Do not let the yardstick become false precision when a qualitative boundary is the honest standard.

source_evidence.source_quote: What yardstick(s) do I use? What is the yardstick by which the decision is made?
How can I easily measure to what degree my goal are being achieved? What indicators can I track?

review_notes.normalization_note: Bevelin candidate overlay outcome: adds a contrast-misreaction treatment to the existing baseline-establishment owner without changing the default V60 artifact. The distinct receiver action is an independent yardstick after removing the adjacent comparison frame.
dropped_material: Did not duplicate broad quantification, multi-criteria comparison, or statistical-discipline records.
Did not add a Lane 1 subpattern in this slice; this is tested first as private V60 substrate.
open_questions: Replay should test whether this enters cases where contrast drives acceptance, and whether it changes product action rather than adding metric theater.

absence_records.reason: The source warns that baselines become dangerous when they fossilize into doctrine after the environment has shifted.

source_evidence.source_quote: the initial baseline fossilizes into doctrine and the team keeps optimizing around an outdated starting frame after the environment has shifted

absence_records.reason: The source rejects a baseline metric that is convenient to measure but weakly tied to the real causal drivers of success or failure.

source_evidence.source_quote: the chosen baseline metric is convenient to measure but weakly tied to the real causal drivers of success or failure

absence_records.reason: The source says goal setting must be separated from solution, diagnosis, and implementation thinking; it does not support a baseline that imports the execution plan into the desired outcome.

source_evidence.source_quote: Goal setting must be done without thinking about solutions
Am I defining the desired outcome, or am I already thinking about the execution steps required to achieve it?
clarity of the starting condition and intentional focus on the desired goal

review_notes.normalization_note: Batch 8 source reading found strong reviewed depth for Baseline Establishment. Extracted one starting-condition comparison gate and rejected stale-baseline and convenient-metric overclaims. PR60 adds a guard against importing solutions or execution steps into the goal baseline.
dropped_material: No runtime, prompt, lane, packet, or user-facing change is implied.
open_questions: Later execution-packet review should check whether this card helps separate baseline facts from success theater.
